title: Arthropteris webbiana
intro: Arthropteris webbiana, also known as the New Zealand fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Lindsaeaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, which include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as serving as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agent. The bioactive constituents of Arthropteris webbiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas and infusions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and salves for skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Arthropteris webbiana is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is native to the island of Madeira. The classification of Arthropteris webbiana is a type of vascular plant, belonging to the kingdom Plantae. More specifically, it is classified under the division Pteridophyta, the class Polypodiopsida, and the order Polypodiales. As a member of the family Polypodiaceae, Arthropteris webbiana is closely related to other species of maidenhair ferns.
